column1: # - severity enum values: P0 / P1 / P2 / P3
#   - P0: Site-wide core functionality unavailable for more than 30 minutes
#   - P1: Core functionality partially unavailable or degraded for more than 15 minutes
#   - P2: Non-core functionality failure or brief fluctuation
# - db_connections_abnormal_start: The time when DB connection count began abnormally rising; this time point is only visible in the Grafana screenshot

column2: number of affected unique orders (deduplicated by order_id)
in CNY. Calculation = affected_orders x average order value for the current month from the SLA Assessment Table
format XX.XX%. Calculation = (total minutes in month - cumulative March incident minutes - this incident's minutes) / total minutes in month x 100%

column1: # - trigger enum values: config_change / code_deploy / infra_failure / third_party / traffic_spike / unknown
# - direct_cause enum values: connection_pool_exhaustion / memory_leak / disk_full / network_partition / third_party_timeout / code_bug / other
# - root_cause enum values: config_change / code_defect / capacity_planning / third_party / operational_error / unknown
# - config_change_detail: Specific description of the change
column2: including parameter name and changed values

column1: # - action_priority enum values: P0 / P1 / P2
# - overall_severity_assessment enum values: critical / major / minor
#   - critical: SLA violated and core functionality impacted for more than 60 minutes
#   - major: Core functionality impacted for 15-60 minutes
# - recurrence_risk enum values: high / medium / low
# - related_historical_incident_id: If a related historical incident is found
column2: fill in the incident ID; otherwise leave blank
must include the specific visible symptoms and concerns reported by users. Information source: customer complaint recording forwarded by customer service
